h1: Coconut Shell Shisha Charcoal: Shapes, Grades & Specifications
heroSubtitle: Coconut shell shisha charcoal briquettes are compressed coconut-shell carbon pressed into uniform shapes for hookah. Also sold as coconut charcoal, hookah coals, shisha cubes, or natural charcoal briquettes — {{brand}} presses {{shapeCount}} shapes in {{gradeCount}} quality grades, factory-direct from {{port}}, {{country}}.

atAGlance.heading: At a glance
atAGlance.definition: Coconut shell shisha charcoal is a quick-light-free briquette made by carbonizing coconut shells, milling the char, binding it with food-grade tapioca starch, and pressing it into a fixed shape. It burns hotter and longer than wood charcoal, leaves less ash, and adds no taste or odor to the tobacco — which is why it is the default fuel for hookah lounges and shisha retail worldwide.

shapes.intro: Every shape below is pressed from the same 100% coconut shell formulation and is available in all {{gradeCount}} quality grades — the grade is chosen per order, not per shape. Sizes are cut to your specification; the dimensions and approximate pieces-per-kilogram listed are our published formats. You can mix shapes and sizes inside a single {{moqLabel}} container.

grades.intro: All three grades are 100% natural coconut shell charcoal — no chemicals, no fillers — produced under {{isoStandard}} and classified for sea transport as IMDG {{unNumber}}. The grades are separated by two things a buyer can measure: ash content and the raw-material selection behind it. The numeric specifications below are factory targets; a per-batch certificate of analysis (COA) issued by {{auditors}} ships with every order.
grades.caption: Factory targets per grade. Independent lab COA issued per shipment; figures confirmed on each batch.
grades.rawMaterialPending: Per-grade source profile pending QC sign-off

ash.body: Ash content is the percentage of mineral residue left after the briquette has fully combusted, measured by mass. It is the single most visible quality marker to your end customer: lower ash means less white debris in the bowl, fewer interruptions to scrape and re-seat coals, and a cleaner-looking session. Our grades run from {{premiumAsh}} on Premium down to {{platinumAsh}} on Platinum. Below roughly 2%, the difference is obvious to an experienced lounge operator — which is why Platinum commands a premium shelf price.

rawMaterial.body: The ash gap between grades comes from the raw material. Denser, more mature coconut shell carbonized at a controlled temperature yields a higher fixed-carbon, lower-ash briquette; younger or mixed shell raises ash and shortens burn. Each grade is therefore built from a different shell selection — the specific per-grade sourcing profile is being finalized with our QC team and will be published here. Read how we select and carbonize shell on the raw-materials page.
rawMaterial.linkLabel: How we source and carbonize coconut shell

shared.body: Some specifications are identical across all three grades, so we state them once rather than repeating them in every column: moisture is held at {{premiumMoisture}}, every grade passes a {{premiumDropTest}} drop test for breakage resistance in transit, and sealed shelf life is {{shelfLifeMonths}} months. All grades are odorless and tasteless when lit, with no quick-light accelerants.

docs.body: Download the primary-source evidence: the independent certificate of analysis, the {{iso9001Short}} certificate, and — once published — the per-grade lab report and material safety data sheet (SDS/MSDS) you will file with your customs broker.
docs.msdsPendingTitle: Material safety data sheet (SDS/MSDS) — pending publication

experience.body: Specifications are not abstract. Lower ash content keeps the bowl clean and the airflow open, so the session draws the same on the last 20 minutes as the first. A longer burn time — {{premiumBurnTime}} on Premium, up to {{platinumBurnTime}} on Platinum — means fewer coal changes per session and less interruption for the customer. A stable peak temperature near {{platinumBurnTemp}} delivers consistent heat to the tobacco without scorching it, and because the briquette is pure carbonized coconut shell with no accelerant, it adds no chemical taste or smell — the flavor your customer pays for is the tobacco, not the coal.

business.body: Consistency is what protects your margin. A coal that burns the same in every box means fewer customer complaints, fewer returns, and a reputation your lounge or retail brand can charge for. Low ash and long burn let you position a premium SKU above the commodity wood-charcoal shelf. And because every batch ships with a {{auditors}} COA, you can answer a distributor's specification question with a document instead of a promise. Compare the coconut-shell advantage against bamboo and wood charcoal in our buyer's guide.

choose.intro: The product is the same coconut shell briquette across every use case — what changes is the shape, size, and packaging that fits how your customers buy and burn it.

cafe.body: High-volume venues burn through coals fast, so the priority is cost-per-session and long burn. Order larger 26–28 mm cubes or flats in bulk master-box packing — the lowest cost per kilogram, minimal retail printing. Platinum or Super Premium grade keeps ash off the table during long sessions.

smokeShop.body: Retail sells on shelf appeal and brand. Choose smaller 25 mm cubes or hexagonals in printed inner boxes ({{innerBoxSizes}}), and consider OEM / white-label so the box carries your brand, not ours. A printed 1 kg box is the standard retail unit.

market.intro: Size preference varies by region. These are common specifications by market — your account manager will confirm the right SKU and the import particulars for your country:

items.label: United States
items.note: 25–26 mm cubes for lounges and retail; flats and fingers for HMD/kaloud users.

items.label: United Kingdom
items.note: 25–26 mm cubes are the retail standard; printed 1 kg inner boxes.

items.label: Saudi Arabia & GCC
items.note: Larger 26–28 mm cubes for long lounge sessions; hexagonals for retail shelf.

items.label: Germany & EU
items.note: 26–28 mm cubes; REACH-compliant and full export documentation.

items.label: Russia & CIS
items.note: 22–25 mm cubes plus finger and hexagonal formats.

newcomer.body: If this is your first container, start with a proven, fast-moving SKU — 25 mm cubes in Super Premium grade — to validate demand before you commit to a custom range. Once the SKU sells, move to private label: the same briquette in a box that carries your brand. Our guides walk through both steps.
newcomer.startBrandLinkLabel: How to start your own charcoal brand

ordering.moqBody: The minimum order is {{moqLabel}} — we load full containers only, but you can mix shapes and grades inside one. Production lead time is {{leadTimeLabel}} from the deposit. Goods load {{portLabel}}.
ordering.incotermsLine: Prices are quoted {{portLabel}}; CIF to your destination port is available on request.
ordering.dgLine: Coconut shell charcoal ships as a regulated dangerous good: {{imdgLabel}}. We file the declaration and the Self-Heating Test report with every booking.
ordering.nextAction: Send your target shape, grade, quantity, and destination port and we will quote the same day in most time zones — or message us on WhatsApp for a faster reply.
